promotions.label: Fitbit data quality in All of Us
promotions.reason: Core domain data: data quality control for Fitbit device data from All of Us, which is a primary dataset used in this project. Directly relevant for understanding wearable sensor data quality in the same data source the project leverages for PA research.

promotions.label: Consumer wearables for PA surveillance
promotions.reason: Core domain data: addresses considerations for consumer-grade wearables in physical activity surveillance, directly relevant to the project's use of wearable step count data for JITAI state estimation and intervention targeting.

promotions.label: Fitbit accuracy systematic review
promotions.reason: Core domain data: systematic review of Fitbit device accuracy provides critical domain knowledge for interpreting step count measurements from the primary wearable sensor used in the project's PA intervention research.

promotions.label: Fitbit sleep accuracy meta-analysis
promotions.reason: Core domain data: meta-analysis of Fitbit sleep stage accuracy is directly relevant since the project's factored MDP includes a sleep state variable derived from wearable data.

promotions.label: Wearable activity tracker accuracy
promotions.reason: Core domain data: comprehensive review of wrist-wearable activity tracking device accuracy informs sensor processing pipeline and measurement reliability for the project's step count and activity outcomes.

promotions.label: Commercial sleep tracker accuracy
promotions.reason: Core domain data: accuracy assessment of commercial wearable sleep tracking devices supports the project's sleep state variable estimation from consumer wearable sensors.

promotions.label: Fitness tracker validity
promotions.reason: Core domain data: validity assessment of mainstream wearable fitness devices provides methodological grounding for the project's primary measurement instrument (wearable step counters).

promotions.label: Activity trackers in Parkinson's
promotions.reason: Core domain data: accuracy assessment of activity trackers in a clinical population informs understanding of wearable performance across subgroups relevant to the project's diverse user populations in PA interventions.

promotions.label: Reactivity to digital measurement
promotions.reason: Theoretical foundation: examines how digital in-the-moment measurement of health behaviour affects behavior itself, a critical consideration for MRT/JITAI design where measurement and intervention are intertwined.

promotions.label: Fitbit data in All of Us (2025)
promotions.reason: Core domain data: directly describes PA and sleep data exploration and processing in the All of Us dataset that the project uses for offline RL and simulation, providing essential data processing methodology.

promotions.label: Wearable data quality challenges
promotions.reason: Core domain data / Implementation-relevant: addresses mitigating data quality issues in ambulatory wrist-worn monitoring, directly relevant to the project's sensor processing pipeline and real-world deployment.

promotions.label: Fitbit data in All of Us (2023)
promotions.reason: Core domain data: explains how Fitbit data are structured and made available in the All of Us dataset, essential methodological reference for the project's primary data source for offline RL and simulation.

promotions.label: Sleep patterns and chronic disease
promotions.reason: Core domain data: uses All of Us wearable data to study sleep patterns, providing domain knowledge about sleep distributions in the same dataset the project uses, relevant for the MDP sleep state dimension.

promotions.label: Machine Learning Methods to Personalize Persuasive Strategies in mHealth Interve
promotions.reason: Directly applicable methodology: scoping review finding RL is the dominant approach for timing personalization in PA mHealth interventions. Provides taxonomy that situates our config-driven framework in the broader landscape of ML-for-PA personalization.

promotions.label: A scoping review of methodologies for applying artificial intelligence to physic
promotions.reason: Directly applicable methodology: comprehensive scoping review finding RL is most promising for adaptive PA intervention delivery and personalization. Directly situates RL-based JITAI methodology in the evidence base.

promotions.label: MoveMentor - examining effectiveness of a machine learning and app-based digital
promotions.reason: Directly applicable methodology: protocol for RCT evaluating ML/RL-based PA message personalization JITAI. Provides important upcoming clinical trial context for comparing RL personalization effectiveness.

promotions.label: Automated Personalized Goal Setting for Individual Exercise Behavior: Protocol f
promotions.reason: Directly applicable methodology: protocol for adaptive intervention trial using contextual bandits for personalized PA goal recommendation. Directly relevant as a concurrent CB-for-PA trial design and comparison point.

promotions.label: The Micro-Randomized Trial for Developing Digital Interventions: Data Analysis M
promotions.reason: Directly applicable methodology: foundational MRT methods paper introducing WCLS estimator for causal excursion effects, using HeartSteps as running example. Essential reference for the project's MRT-based experimental design framework.

promotions.label: Multi-Level Micro-Randomized Trial: Detecting the Proximal Effect of Messages on
promotions.reason: Directly applicable methodology: extends MRT design to multi-level intervention components, motivated by PA intervention for diabetes. Relevant for the project's multi-action (4-action) JITAI design with varying treatment levels.

promotions.label: Assessing Time-Varying Causal Effect Moderation in the Presence of Cluster-Level
promotions.reason: Directly applicable methodology: extends MRT causal framework to handle cluster-level heterogeneity and interference, relevant for the project's multi-user deployment scenarios and group-level analyses.

promotions.label: The Micro-Randomized Trial for Developing Digital Interventions: Experimental De
promotions.reason: Directly applicable methodology: comprehensive MRT tutorial covering design, implementation, and analysis with HeartSteps examples. Definitive practitioner guide for the project's experimental methodology.

promotions.label: Estimating Time-Varying Direct and Indirect Causal Excursion Effects with Longit
promotions.reason: Directly applicable methodology: extends MRT causal framework to decompose direct and indirect effects through time-varying mediators, relevant for understanding mechanism of action in the project's multi-action interventions.

promotions.label: A Meta-Learning Method for Estimation of Causal Excursion Effects to Assess Time
promotions.reason: Directly applicable methodology: doubly robust DR-WCLS estimator handling unknown randomization, missing data, and high-dimensional mHealth features. Key advance for robust analysis in the project's real-world deployment context.

promotions.label: Incorporating Auxiliary Variables to Improve the Efficiency of Time-Varying Trea
promotions.reason: Directly applicable methodology: method to incorporate auxiliary sensor variables into MRT estimation. Directly relevant for leveraging the project's rich wearable sensor data to improve treatment effect estimation efficiency.

promotions.label: Estimating Causal Effects for Binary Outcomes Using Per-Decision Inverse Probabi
promotions.reason: Directly applicable methodology: per-decision IPW estimators reducing variance for binary proximal outcomes common in mHealth (e.g., engagement, app opens). Relevant for the project's binary outcome analysis needs.

promotions.label: Incorporating nonparametric methods for estimating causal excursion effects in m
promotions.reason: Directly applicable methodology: extends causal excursion effects to zero-inflated count outcomes, relevant for the project's step count outcomes which exhibit zero-inflation (periods of non-wear or inactivity).

promotions.label: Efficient and Globally Robust Causal Excursion Effect Estimation
promotions.reason: Directly applicable methodology: derives semiparametric efficiency bound for causal excursion effects and proposes estimators achieving it. Establishes theoretical ceiling for the project's MRT analysis methods.

promotions.label: Data integration methods for micro-randomized trials
promotions.reason: Directly applicable methodology: methods to integrate data from multiple MRTs for efficiency gains, relevant for the project's goal of combining multiple PA intervention trials or study phases.

promotions.label: Modeling Time-Varying Effects of Mobile Health Interventions Using Longitudinal
promotions.reason: Directly applicable methodology: first semiparametric varying-coefficient model for time-varying MRT effects using longitudinal step count outcomes. HeartSteps analysis shows prompt effects vary by context. Directly relevant to project context.

promotions.label: Doubly Robust Estimation of Causal Excursion Effects in Micro-Randomized Trials
promotions.reason: Directly applicable methodology: first doubly robust CEE estimator handling MAR missing data in MRTs, demonstrated on HeartSteps data. Addresses sensor non-wear and missed self-report issues pervasive in the project's deployment context.

promotions.label: Evaluation of the HeartSteps Online Sampling Algorithm
promotions.reason: Directly applicable methodology: case study of HeartSteps V2/V3 online randomization algorithm managing intervention frequency and uniformity constraints. Directly relevant to the project's HeartSteps reproduction and constraint handling in MRTs.

promotions.label: Distal Causal Excursion Effects: Modeling Long-Term Effects of Time-Varying Trea
promotions.reason: Directly applicable methodology: introduces distal causal excursion effects for long-term behavior change in MRTs, with HeartSteps analysis. Bridges proximal effects to long-term outcomes critical for the project's behavior change goals.

demotions.label: A novel physical activity recognition approach using deep ensemble optimized tra
demotions.reason: Tangential domain: uses RL purely as a model optimization technique (ensemble weighting) for activity recognition, not for intervention design or personalization. Does not address JITAIs, MRTs, or adaptive intervention delivery, which are the core of this project.

demotions.label: Personalized Type 1 Diabetes Management: Reinforcement Learning-Based Insulin Do
demotions.reason: Tangential domain: focused on insulin dosing for diabetes management where PA is only a context variable, not an intervention target. The goal is glucose control, not PA promotion. The core methodology and objective are outside the project's scope.

demotions.label: Artificial Intelligence-Enabled Mobile Health Intervention (mDiabetes) to Reduce
demotions.reason: Too general: uses rule-based AI message personalization (not RL), evaluated via pre-post design (not MRT/MDP). Lacks the RL methodology, config-driven design, or JITAI framework that defines this project's approach. AI-personalized messaging without RL specificity.
